title: QUIC RFC 9000 ECN ACK Count Validation Work Item
summary: Carve direct proof, fuzz topoffs, and permanent benchmark coverage for the helper-backed RFC 9000 ECN ACK-count validation floor without claiming broader endpoint ECN deployment.
planned_changes:
- Add focused architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts for the ECN ACK-count validation helper.
- Link the involved RFC 9000 requirements to the focused artifact set while preserving the broad RFC9000-0001 trace.
- Fill empty requirement homes for missing-count, zeroed-feedback, ECT(0)+CE, ECT(1)+CE, exceed-sent, never-applied ECT, revalidation, and later-failure disable behavior.
- Add deterministic fuzz topoffs for representative invalid ACK ECN count deltas and reordered ACK frames.
- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for successful and failing ECN validation branches.
- Preserve focused verification evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-ecn-ack-count-validation/`.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 ECN validation requirement-home tests, run the new `QuicEcnValidationBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-ecn-ack-count-validation/`.
out_of_scope: Endpoint ECN enablement or policy selection.
Setting ECT codepoints in IP headers.
Receiver-side ECN count generation from IP headers.
Migration-triggered ECN probing or path-wide ECN deployment.
Public API migration or ECN controls.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof, fuzz, and benchmark topoff over the existing ECN validation helper; no production behavior changed.
